channel.title: nexusEVnews
channel.description: Independent EV and renewable energy news, curated daily. Electric vehicles, solar, battery tech, and charging infrastructure.

item.title: CATL at Auto China: Superfast Charging, 99-Second Battery Swaps, and the Whole Ecosystem Behind It
item.dc:creator: Kim Java
item.description: Kim Java flew to Auto China to see CATL's Shenxing III LFP battery, which charges from 10 to 80 percent in under four minutes, alongside the Qilin 3 NCM pack and a battery swap network that processes a full pack change in 99 seconds.

item.title: EV Adoption Is Outrunning Oil Prices. New Research Explains Why.
item.dc:creator: Just Have a Think
item.description: A London School of Economics paper published in March 2025 argues that EV adoption is now driven by systemic, path-dependent forces, shaped by infrastructure, policy, and self-reinforcing feedback loops rather than oil price movements alone.

item.title: 2026 Polestar 3: More Power, Faster Charging, Slightly Less Range
item.dc:creator: Autotrader
item.description: The updated Polestar 3 gains 800V architecture, 350 kW charging, and 536 bhp from revised dual motors. The trade-off is a smaller battery and a maximum range of 394 miles, down from the previous long-range model.

item.title: China's Best-Selling Car Is Coming to Europe: First Look at the Geely EX2
item.dc:creator: Electrifying
item.description: The Geely EX2 outsold every other car in China last year with around 465,000 units. It arrives in the UK in late 2026 priced from around £20,000, with a 375-litre boot, a 75-litre frunk, and a choice of 30 or 40 kWh batteries.

item.title: One Year of Solar and Battery: Under £3,000 Saved, 6.4 Years to Break Even
item.dc:creator: Andrew Till / Mr. EV
item.description: After 12 months with 30 solar panels and two Alpha batteries, a UK homeowner saved just under £3,000. The payback on an £18,990 system works out to 6.4 years, better than the installer's original 7 to 8 year estimate.

item.title: Atlanta, Rivian, and the Quiet Coincidence
item.dc:creator: Jacob Hunka
item.description: Atlanta has a failing air quality grade and a Rivian plant on its doorstep. A hypothetical about what one city could do with the electric mobility infrastructure that already exists — R2 autonomous taxis, ALSO cargo quads, e-bikes, and ZTL-style restricted zones — and what the health data says the stakes actually are. Includes data from the HEI lockdown air quality study (Chen et al., 2025) and the American Lung Association's 2025 Atlanta air quality report.

item.title: One Wheel, 1,000 Horsepower: Inside the YASA In-Wheel Motor System
item.dc:creator: Ziroth
item.description: YASA and Drive System Design built an in-wheel system delivering 750 kW peak and 400 kW continuous that weighs the same as the brakes, upright, and wheel bearings it replaces. The 12-kilogram axial flux motor pairs with a 6.2:1 planetary gearbox, and the assembly is validated under 3G cornering loads using a hydraulic multi-axis test rig.

item.title: Off the Grid on $25,500: Running a Full House, an EV, and a Bitcoin Miner on Solar
item.dc:creator: Minute Man Solar
item.description: An EcoFlow Delta Pro Ultra X with six battery modules, 23 bifacial panels on portable stands, and a grid interlock kit runs a full California house, a Rivian R1S, a Bitcoin miner, and a 3D print farm for $25,500. No permits required for the temporary panel setup.

item.title: Three Wheels, Solar Panels, and a 2026 Delivery Target: Inside Aptera's Factory
item.dc:creator: Realistick
item.description: Aptera's CEO walked through the Carlsbad factory ahead of a planned end-of-2026 launch. The three-wheeled solar EV targets 100 Wh per mile on a 43 kWh pack, uses a 150 kW Vitesco motor, and is priced at $40,000 at launch.

item.title: Nico Müller Wins in Berlin at the 69th Attempt, on Porsche's 75th Motorsport Anniversary
item.dc:creator: Formula E
item.description: After 69 career starts, Nico Müller took his first Formula E victory at the Berlin E-Prix, driving for Porsche on the team's 75th motorsport anniversary. Eduardo Mortara leads the drivers championship by four points after round 7 of season 12.

item.title: BYD Flash Charging Gets a Real-World Test, and It Holds Up
item.dc:creator: Batteries Included Podcast
item.description: Kyle Conner tested BYD flash charging on a crashed, unprepped Denza in China and completed 10 to 80 percent in approximately seven minutes. BYD has 200 stations live in China and 2,500 more units ready to activate.

item.title: How Pancake-Shaped Motors Could Cut 200 kg from an Electric Car
item.dc:creator: Everything Electric TECH
item.description: YASA's axial flux motors are about 20% as thick as a conventional unit and weigh roughly a quarter as much, yet deliver three to four times the power density. Their Oxford factory ships 25,000 units a year. One prototype produced 1,000 horsepower from a motor weighing 12.7 kilograms. Mercedes-Benz acquired YASA in 2021.

item.title: XPeng's City Driving System Takes on FSD in Beijing Traffic
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Reviews
item.description: Kyle Conner drove Tesla FSD in China for 45 minutes, then ran XPeng VLA 2.0 on public roads the same day. The P7 Ultra costs around 300,000 RMB ($42,000 USD), charges at roughly 500 kW, makes 600 horsepower, and uses cameras and radar without lidar.

item.title: This Netherlands E-Bike Commuter Hits 53 km/h on a 1,000W Mid-Drive
item.dc:creator: FLATLANDER TECH
item.description: A Netherlands-based builder converts a standard bike with a Tongsheng 1,000W mid-drive motor, 52V battery tucked into rear panniers, and a 3D-printed phone mount. Cruising speed with the current cassette: 53 km/h. The frame looks unmodified until you look closely.

item.title: Tesla Merges FSD, Smart Summon, and Robotaxi onto One Neural Network
item.dc:creator: Ryan Shaw
item.description: FSD 14.3.2 puts all three driving systems on a shared model. Also this week: Mercedes removes lidar from its 2027 C-Class and CLA EVs, Tesla Semi production confirmed at 50,000 units per year, and one Illinois driver cut his Tesla insurance bill by 86% using Safety Score 3.0.

item.title: BMW iX3 vs Mercedes GLC Electric: Which German SUV Gets the Reset Right?
item.dc:creator: CAR magazine
item.description: Two days of back-to-back driving. The GLC Electric rides on air suspension, packs a 39.1-inch hyperscreen, and claims 406 miles. The iX3 claims 500 miles, charges faster at 400 kW, and handles better. Boot space: identical at 520 litres. Real-world consumption: nearly equal.

item.title: Scout Traveler and Terra in Person: 500 Miles, Body-on-Frame, and Under $60,000
item.dc:creator: Gjeebs
item.description: The Scout Traveler is a full-size EREV SUV with 350 miles of battery range and a built-in generator that extends total range to 500 miles. The Terra truck seats six with a front bench. Both start under $60,000. Software is co-developed with Rivian. Production is targeted for 2027 with customer deliveries in 2028. Body-on-frame, solid rear axle, NACS charging port, and a direct-to-consumer sales model. Manufactured in South Carolina.

item.title: The NIO ET9 in Beijing: 925V, 600 kW Charging, and a Suspension You Can Watch from the Back Seat
item.dc:creator: Out of Spec Reviews
item.description: Out of Spec Reviews drove the NIO ET9 through Beijing traffic and an early-morning parking lot session. The $100,000 luxury sedan runs on a fully in-house 925V electrical architecture with active suspension, steer-by-wire, rear steer, and DC charging approaching 600 kW. Battery swap takes about 3 minutes. Dual rear seat screens display live suspension power consumption. Not sold outside China. Main criticisms: steer-by-wire response slower than Cybertruck, and active suspension did not fully absorb initial bump impacts.

item.title: New EVs Now Average Less Than New Petrol Cars in the UK, and the Numbers Have a Catch
item.dc:creator: Autotrader
item.description: Autotrader UK data shows the average new EV price has fallen £785 below the average new petrol car price for the first time. The crossover is entirely discount-driven: without incentives, EVs are still around 15 percent more expensive. The UK government EV grant contributes up to £3,750 off qualifying vehicles under £37,000 through 2028. Battery costs have fallen 93 percent since 2010. The Renault 5 was the most inquired EV on the platform in April 2026. The VW Golf was the most popular car overall.

item.title: Rivian Q1 2026: R2 Production Starts, Georgia Capacity Raised to 300,000 Units, Guidance Intact
item.dc:creator: Travis Ketchum
item.description: Rivian delivered 10,365 vehicles in Q1 2026 and posted $1.4 billion in revenue at a 9 percent gross margin. Software and services generated $473 million, up 49 percent year over year, with $282 million from the Volkswagen joint venture. Georgia plant first-phase capacity was raised from 200,000 to 300,000 annual units. Cash on hand: $4.8 billion. A DOE loan of up to $4.5 billion covers Georgia construction. Full-year delivery guidance of 62,000 to 67,000 vehicles is unchanged. Public R2 deliveries expected around June. RAP1 autonomy chip on track.
